title: Infer hidden SC/VSC pit windows from synchronized field slowdown
summary: The Virtual Strategy Engineer paper shows pit loss drops materially during full-course yellow phases and that teams react immediately.
rationale: The Virtual Strategy Engineer paper shows pit loss drops materially during full-course yellow phases and that teams react immediately. Since your dataset lacks explicit SC/VSC flags, a latent caution detector can recover a major missing strategic state from race-wide synchronization patterns.
implementation_hint: At each `Race-Year` × `LapNumber`, compute field-level features such as median and upper-quantile `LapTime_Delta`, fraction of drivers with large positive deltas, abnormal simultaneous `PitStop` spikes, and position churn; join those race-lap features back to each row and interact them with tyre-age and laps-remaining features.
expected_effect: Should catch caution-driven pit waves and improve rows where the individual driver state looks weak but the whole field is entering a discounted stop window.
risk: Same-lap aggregation is safe, but anything using future laps is not; race-wide anomalies may also reflect weather or red-flag artifacts rather than SC/VSC only.
